# Subwise

> Subwise is India's financial-reminder layer, built on WhatsApp. Connect Gmail once and Subwise
> organizes every recurring payment — subscriptions, free trials, rent, EMIs, insurance, SIPs,
> utilities, credit cards — then reminds you on WhatsApp before money leaves your account. Free.

## What Subwise does
- Scans Gmail (read-only) to auto-detect subscriptions, free trials, and auto-pay (UPI AutoPay / e-NACH / card SI / bank e-mandate) mandates.
- Lets you add any recurring commitment by hand (rent, EMI, insurance, SIP, custom) with a flexible schedule.
- Sends reminders on WhatsApp 1, 3, or 7 days before a charge — the channel Indians actually check.
- Shows a financial overview: monthly & yearly commitment, what's due this week, trials ending, spend by category.
- Helps you cancel — including the India-specific UPI auto-pay mandate, which cancelling the app alone does not stop.

## Who it's for
Working professionals in India (22–35) who lose money to forgotten subscriptions and silent auto-debits.

## Key facts
- Free. Privacy-first: read-only Gmail, only payment-related emails, never attachments, never sold.
- Or sign up with email/password and skip Gmail entirely (manual tracking).
- WhatsApp reminders are official (Meta WhatsApp Business Platform). Reply STOP to opt out anytime (TRAI compliant).

## Pages
- Home: https://subwise.co.in/
- Cancellation guides (incl. UPI mandate): https://subwise.co.in/guides
- Privacy: https://subwise.co.in/privacy
- Terms: https://subwise.co.in/terms
